Another value which was not very popular amongst the sample is after sale service, whereby it was chosen by  12.3 % of the respondents a s  least important when shopping. The reason for this may be that majority were buying from mass market ,  which does not have after sale or refund policies for worn items.  Therefore, to summarize the findings, the most important aspects for customers of fashion brands were: quality  and  variety.  Fashion companies need to focus on providing high quality clothes, convenience for the segment which requires it and variety both in western and eastern options  t o cater to the diversity of preferences.   Experiential values For sensory values,  only a certain portion  of the sample felt that social media presence of company is   important as they may see the photos and videos of the products (important for  5.2 %) and thus satisfy their sensory values.  Secondly, entertainment through digital content of the company was not very important . This was reinforced in the result that social media presence of the fashion companies wasn’t a deciding factor in purchasing decision.    Furthermore, innovation and novelty were  more  important  i.e.,  for 2 2 .4%, and thus they choose to buy different products e.g., western and eastern wear from different companies. This is shown in the wide spread of choice of sample when choosing where to buy from. Additionally, wellness was not a major priority for the respondents when buying from fashion brands as they focus more on quality, variety and the product itself, rather than the benefit it provides.  Authenticity was  also not as   crucial as  just for 1/5 of the customers  felt it is important when choosing a brand.   Sustainable practices of fashion companies were  the most  important to more than half of the sample i.e., 51.2%. This illustrates the growing awareness and call for action by customers to change the business practices in the industry.  Lastly, o nly  6.9 % of the customers said that social media presence of brands is important in their purchase decision, therefore indicating that majority of the sample bought products for  their appearance and price, rather than being influenced by the  social media marketing of  companies.  However, for the ones that it is important, it is a significant aspect in their brand loyalty and therefore is crucial source of information about products and brand.  Therefore, companies should focus on their sustainable practices and offer new designs and innovate with their products to attract customers. In order to cater for the diverse needs of customers, companies should provide high quality clothes with greater variety. Lastly, even though social media wasn’t the most important value, it may still be used as an effective tool for customer engagement and focus on their target market which finds it important. Symbolic values Self-identity was not as important and was categorized as one of the least important aspects when shopping by 17.5%. Thus, the choice of product  bought by customers  is not impacted by this a lot.  This may imply that customers do not emphasize on expression of individual identity through the clothing they wear  and prioritize other factors.  Moreover, conspicuous consumption and buying brands for status was not very important for customers.  This indicates a gradual shift of the Pakistan’s customer towards a more conscious and sustainable consumption pattern and they would rather purchase clothing which align with their values and needs.  Thus, customers are not very willing to pay a price premium for branded products.  Cost values Price of the product i.e., the economic cost was very important for  75.9 % of the surveyed customers,  which is illustrated in the fact that there is not a very high brand loyalty. Moreover, psychological costs i.e., saving time and making life easier was not  the most important (25.9%)  as customers wish to enjoy their time shopping. Lastly, risk aversion was also least important and only  5.2 % have it as the priority when shopping.  Therefore, the customers according to the result are primarily price-sensitive and did not illustrate high brand loyalty. This implies that fashion companies need to focus on their prices to be competitive in the market and retain customers in the current economic situation. Moreover, as customers prioritize spending time shopping, it illustrates that companies need to have pleasant and convenient shopping experience.  To sum up, the most important values for fashion  companies’  customers in Pakistan were  the ones  mentioned in  Fig. 2.2.2. , with quality and price being at the top.  More customers wish to  buy products which are of good quality as they possess characteristics of durability and longevity, therefore they are meant to last. This provides the customers with higher value and therefore more satisfaction. Due to the lower disposable income, the customers in Pakistan wish to take into account the quality as well as price so as the apparel withstands the constant wear and tear, has a longer life span, thus requiring less replacements, helping customers save money in the long run.  I believe these will continue to be important in the future as well as customers wish to get high quality products.  Whereas the least important are identified on  Fig. 2.2.3 , where conspicuou s consumption and psychological costs take the lead.  As noted from the questionnaire, customers of Pakistan’s fashion companies are not as much specific about conspicuous consumption i.e., buying brands to illustrate their status. The reason for this trend may be again associated with the economic situation currently, whereby customers are aiming at  to an extent lessening  their expenses on  products which are not necessities such as clothing. I believe that as the economic situation stabilizes, these may not be the least important factors as people wish to be associated with certain luxury brands and use their products as status symbols, and it is an important characteristic of a specific demographic segment.  Fig 2.2. 2 .:   Most important values for customers Source:  Questionnaire distributed to Pakistan’s customers (Done by author) Fig 2.2. 2 .:   Less important values for customers Source:  Questionnaire distributed to Pakistan’s customers (Done by author) 3 . 2   Fashion companies value chain: adaptation to Pakistan customer values   With the determined findings stated before, certain managerial implications be outlined for Pakistan’s fashion companies. In order to determine these implications, the opportunities and risks have been assessed from the information collected  about the customer values, the external market analysis and data gathered from interviews with company representatives  through the stages of value chain of companies .  In each of the pertinent value chain stage of the business, opportunities and/or risks have been identified, and with each of them the  implications   for companies and what to take into account  have been provided.  Inbound logistics : Opportunities  Sustainability  As has been highlighted in through the result of questionnaire about Pakistan’s customer values, more than half of the respondents believe sustainable practices of companies are important when taking purchase decisions. This is increasingly crucial for Gen Z and Millennials, as per IBM study of 2022. Sustainability and environmentally friendly practices act as an opportunity for the fashion companies, and they may implement it in various stages of their value chain: Firstly, companies may produce sustainable products by selecting material which is more sustainable e.g., organic cotton, organic hemp, organic linen, Tencel etc. as they are known for having lower environmental impact than others, more conventional materials. Organic cotton is grown from non-GMO seeds, organic hemp as it requires lower water consumption, organic linen as it doesn’t require fertilizers and is biodegradable and lastly Tencel as it is an eco-friendly fabric made with natural linen (Batista, 2021). In case the company does not produce the fabric themselves, they may collaborate with suppliers which produce these specific fabrics. E-commerce Effective inventory management is crucial for controlling and optimizing the inventory levels of any fashion company. With this, companies can forecast their future demand and enable them to determine the inventory levels they will need to meet customer demand without problem of stockpiling or stockout. Moreover, idea of stock replenishment is useful for fashion companies with automated inventory tracking, which allows the purchase order to be placed automatically when stock level reduces beyond a set level. This enables to maintain high level of customer satisfaction and builds their trust with the brand, helping improve retention, increasing profitability (Apparel Magic, 2023). Additionally, having several warehouses in different parts of the country may help the companies optimize their delivery of orders. Increased variety To increase the variety , fashion companies may opt for diversifying their suppliers and seeking new ones if they do not produce the fabric in house or opt for a greater range of fabrics, materials and components if they produce it themselves. In both the cases, company may collaborate with suppliers who are specialists in niche products, thus adding diversity to product range. Innovation One way to innovate the product offerings for customers is to explore new options of fabrics. Fashion companies may seek innovative materials which are sustainable and new for customers. One of the options is leather alternatives such Mirum (leather produced from plants and minerals). (Nast, 2022). Further to that, there may be recycling of textiles to prevent waste disposal, re-use of textile products, repurposing of discarded clothes to adopt the material and converting it into a useful product (Abbas and  Halog , 2021).  Risks Economic instability  During the current period of economic instability in Pakistan, fashion companies may face certain challenges in form of increasing prices of raw materials, the transportation costs etc., which would lead to higher expenses of sourcing the materials for apparel production. In order to reduce this risk, companies may work with suppliers to improve the forecasting of orders and improve the production schedules thereby. Nowadays, companies may make use of machine learning and deep learning algorithms to manage the supply chain planning tasks. According to  Cihat   Polat  (2008), the statistical forecasting methods including artificial intelligence e.g., Neural Networks and Genetic Algorithms and judgmental methods may be used by marketers to take into account the economic situation. These help to improve accuracy in predictions of future demand and thus help reduce chances of over or understocking their products.  Competitive pricing pressure With the economic instability, rising and competitive pricing from other competitors may influence the sourcing and procurement of raw materials. In case the fashion company has a supplier and they do not produce the fabric themselves, increase in prices due to the conditions in market would lead to higher costs for the company, potentially harming the profitability. In this case, the brands may conduct cost analysis to determine how the price increase of fabric would impact the company’s overall cost structure. In case it is more than expected, the company may renegotiate the contract with existing suppliers and/or diversify the supplier base to have more competitive pricing in the market. Operations : Opportunities Sustainability  At this stage, fashion companies may choose to implement measures to reduce waste. This may be done by designing apparel in a way which minimizes waste during production. Further to that, as far as recycling is concerned, companies may choose either of these two types of fabric recycling: mechanical and chemical ones (Dissanayake and Weerasinghe (2021). Mechanical recycling is done by breaking down the fabric into fibers and then converting them into a new textile, whereas chemical recycling uses chemicals to transform the waste into the original shape and then produce new fibers with it. Thus, fashion brands would ensure sustainable resource management, reduce their waste and burden to landfills and therefore mitigate their environmental pollution. Through these practices, companies would ensure their compliance with regulations set by Government in National Environmental Quality Standards and others. Increased variety Introducing new technologies such as Computer aided design (CAD) or computer added manufacturing (CAM) if not done before may efficiently produce more designs and styles for the fashion garments, therefore providing customers with greater choice. Moreover, as size inclusivity is not used by many fashion brands, implementing it and introducing the same designs of clothing (western and eastern styles) would help companies to tap into underserved markets by catering to diverse body types. This would lead to enhancement of customer satisfaction and provide competitive edge to the ones which do as size inclusivity is not a concept widely adopted. As this is not a passing trend, brands may position themselves for a sustained success.    Additionally, as Pakistani market has a specificity of selling stitched and unstitched clothes in the eastern wear categories, introducing the clothes which are unstitched in their stitched versions would be a great opportunity for brands as an identified pain point for customers is that the price of getting the unstitched clothes stitched by tailors is also increasing with the current economic conditions.  Risks Competitive pricing pressure With increased pricing pressure, the production process may be impacted. As companies aim to maintain profitability, some may be compelled to cut costs, which may cause a decrease in quality, harming the brand image.  In such a situation, a cost quality analysis ought to be performed in order to see what are the implications of cutting their costs. This provides a good measure for managers to identify and measure the quality costs and determine which areas allow the company to reduce their costs and which don’t, in order to not compromise the customer satisfaction and brand image. Increasing prices of electricity  The rising prices would directly impact operational costs of companies. As of January 2023, the cost of production as per owners of textile mills increased 100% during the past half a year with rise in electricity tariff (The Nation, 2023). As the manufacturing is heavily reliant on electricity for machinery and equipment and lighting of facilities, this may strain production budgets and financial health. Therefore, the optimal option for these brands would be to implement energy efficiency measures e.g., use renewable energy sources in form of solar panels as mentioned earlier. This would help them offset any reliance on the electricity grid in the longer run. In the shorter run they may also make use of energy management systems which monitor and control the use of energy in the premises in real time and thus reduce the costs. Outbound logistics : Opportunities Sustainability  Fashion companies in Pakistan may further the use of sustainability in their packaging. With the example of Sapphire, other companies may also optimize their packaging by using recycled cloth for producing tote bags instead of plastic or cardboard bags or make use of compostable packaging materials as much as possible. The idea of waste disposal reduced to minimum by producing ‘seed bags’ as was done by Sapphire in the past is innovative and implementable as these were bags which could be torn and planted in soil as they were infused with seeds. This way not only are the bags living up to the aspect of ‘no waste’ but also benefiting the environment as they result in new plants.  Moreover, fashion companies are recommended to implement environmentally friendly practices in warehousing as well. The implementation of ‘green warehousing’. As warehouse buildings contribute most to consumption of energy, as they need energy throughout the life cycle (Rai et al, 2011). Thus, by adjusting the lighting and heating systems of the warehouse, it may account for the energy efficiency initiatives ( Colicchia  et al., 2013). By utilizing energy efficient lighting systems e.g., solar panels, as Pakistan has approximately 300 days per year of sunshine ( Uflat  et al., 2012).  E-commerce With the e-commerce practices of a fashion company, it is important that an efficient order placement, tracking and return process is in place. After the order has been placed, customers need to be provided with a way to monitor the progress and status of their orders in real time. This way,  fashion companies may make it easier for customers to use e-commerce as the anxiety is reduced and their inquiries about status may be resolved by tracking numbers, notifications about dispatchment, delivery etc. All these factors improve overall customer experience and satisfaction. The important characteristics for brands to focus on in this is: Simple purchasing process  As Amazon’s philosophy states, good systems must be in place to address customers complaints and questions, but company should work to avoid customers using them (Economist Intelligence Unit, 2014).  Ease and speed of purchase Customers wish to place orders easily and quickly. Personalized experience Customers generally expect personalized approach. About 71% of respondents were frustrated with impersonal experience during shopping and 91% of consumers have said that they would like to shop with brands which provide offers and recommendations ( Morgan , 2020).  3.  Increased variety The efficiency of logistics operations is crucial in determining the level of competitiveness of a company ( Rouwenhorst  et al., 2000). Therefore, implementing a system e.g., Warehouse Management System (WMS) in fashion companies would allow the management to increase the efficiency and reduce costs, in comparison to using a manual management system ( Atieh  et al., 2016). Other than that, brands may collaborate with third party logistic companies, helping them efficiently transport and deliver products to customers in different locations. Lastly, if the size of the business is considerable, several warehouses may be set up in different regions/ provinces in Pakistan where the demand is highest amongst customers and thus deliver the products at a faster speed with use of the courier services.  Risks Economic instability As seen in the PESTLE analysis and the interviews, more customers are buying clothes on discounted prices, thus companies may need to focus on these sales and promotional offers to  entice their clients. This will work on attracting the price sensitive customers who are more careful with spending their lower disposable income. Thus, they may keep their revenue high by reducing the profit as they sell a higher quantity of goods. Marketing and Sales : Opportunities Sustainability  In the marketing campaigns, companies may highlight and promote the sustainability and ethical practices such as the use of organic cotton, recycled materials, the use of green technology, accreditations or certificates such as that of WRAP compliance. Moreover, the use of sustainable packaging is also a key aspect to be highlighted in the marketing activities. Additionally, it is as important to educate customers about the environmental impact and importance of making sustainable choices. This may be done by posting content on social media to educate customers or hold sustainability focused events in order to foster a conscious decision making. Last, but not the least, collaborating with organizations which aim to amplify the sustainability in the society, such as NGOs or companies which aim to empower global textile producers and communities such as ‘Good Earth Cotton’, is an effective strategy to organize campaigns jointly and promote sustainable fashion practices to raise awareness amongst customers about environmental issues.  E-commerce For the marketing and sales activities of the company to be successful in e-commerce, it is crucial that they have a user-friendly website design. After analyzing several articles (Singh, 2013;  Mediaboom , 2022) about what the characteristics of a website for a fashion company should be like, the following list was drawn up: A visually appealing design The website of a fashion company has to be visually attractive and engaging for customers to reflect the aesthetics and style of the brand through high quality pictures and visually appealing layouts. A mobile friendly interface As the number of customers using mobile devices for shopping is increasing in Pakistan and e-commerce market is expected to grow 6.23% till 2027 (Statista, 2023), it is important to have a web design which can adapt to different devices: desktop, smartphones, tablets etc. A compelling presentation of products A fashion website requires the product images to be of high quality, there is detailed description of the product, its material, the sizes, possibility to zoom in and also provide a 360-degree view to enhance the shopping experience. The last point serves as an opportunity for the brands as it is not widely practiced amongst Pakistan’s fashion brands. Easy navigation To be user-friendly, the visitors need to be able to go through the website with minimal effort, for which the menu, searches and all categories should be easy to find for users. Fast-loading websites By optimizing the page’s loading speed with caches, image compression etc. the company may increase customer satisfaction and decrease possible bounce rates. Easy checkout process Once a customer is ready to purchase, the process of checkout has to go smooth with minimal steps including secure payment options for the purchase to be done without any hassle. Moreover,  as was mentioned by the representative of Sapphire, more customers are buying during promotions and discounts, thus it may serve as an effective marketing strategy to attract customers and improve sales. The overall experience of customers during this may be enhanced by providing personalized offers by using the customer data and tailoring their previously collected individual preferences to enhance the relevance of offers given on website. Furthermore, the communication of these promotions is important through the various channels such as social media pages, their email newsletters to customers who are subscribed, the website etc. All of these tactics would enhance the customer engagement and lead to higher success of the promotion. Digital marketing Utilizing the benefits of digital marketing in the time of growing social media use in Pakistan is an opportunity all fashion brands can work on. As of 2023, the number of internet users in Pakistan increased by 4.4 million (5.4%) since 2022 (Data  Reportal , 2023), and this is only  going to grow in the future. Therefore, the brands may adopt a number of strategies and enhance their marketing efforts in following aspects: Omni channel marketing Nowadays before making a purchase, customers majorly look for and compare different channels through which they access a particular brand (Lorenzo-Romero et al., 2020). Therefore, to make sure the client has the ultimate shopping experience, companies need to integrate different channels This involves a strong brand presence on social media, e-commerce, mobile app etc. By being consistent in all these, and integrating it with offline marketing efforts, fashion companies may drive customer engagement and therefore sales revenue. Social media marketing Social media is an essential component of a brand’s marketing strategy nowadays. Therefore, the fashion companies need to regularly post content on their social media platforms such as Instagram, Facebook, YouTube etc. to increase brand awareness. As per a study, in 2022, 31.5% of the total population of Pakistan ( Oosga , 2023) were active social media users. The most popular platforms amongst them for 2022 were Facebook, Instagram and TikTok. Thus, posting regularly on these platforms’ high-quality images and updates of fashion products generate customer interest. To create a fonder connection, brands may also encourage user generated content (UGC) through hashtags, photos shared on social media of customers, comments on posts and feedback to demonstrate their attention.  Influencer marketing It is known that many customers, before making a purchase, visit blogs or forums to read about what other customers think about a certain product they are interested in. In many instances, these sites or social media pages are of influencers, where they post about whether they were satisfied with purchased product (Lorenzo-Romero et al., 2023). For this, companies need to identify popular fashion influencers in Pakistan whose aesthetic is similar to the brand and collaborate to create sponsored content, endorsement of the brand, reviews of products etc. so the brand resonates with the target audience more. This would allow company to have targeted messaging.  Increased variety Just like other aspects of a company in fashion, marketing strategies have to be adopted by keeping in mind the preferences of each segment. Market segmentation is important to develop highly effective marketing campaigns, higher ROI and satisfy a variety of customers’ needs by offering different promotional activities. For instance, in demographic segmentation, fashion companies in Pakistan may use traditional marketing more for customers in higher age brackets e.g., 45- 60, and digital marketing intensively for age e.g., 16-30. Moreover, conducting regular market research in form of customer surveys may provide valuable insights into their wants and thus be used to cater to the specific segments. Thus, with data analytics and customer relationship management, brands may create personalized recommendations and offers. Innovation Looking at all the implications  above for marketing would result in fashion companies driving their business towards innovation. As features such as VR or AR are not used significantly and artificial intelligence has just become the talk of the day for marketing campaigns, utilizing their benefits may provide brands with innovative ways of interacting with its customers.  Risks Economic instability As with low disposable income Pakistan’s customers are becoming more price sensitive, companies may adopt innovative marketing strategies such as loyalty programs, collaborations with influencers and digital marketing activities enhancement. All of these activities should be tied to prioritizing the existing customers and rewarding their devotion to a brand through loyalty programs may help company. Moreover, generating publicity with a compelling story-telling of brand may positively influence brand loyalty as customers are very selective with their spending during such difficult times. This risk may be seen as an opportunity for brands as well as in these times companies may introduce a new collection of apparel which are lower priced than their regular ones, but have the  same aesthetic and quality as that of other clothes. This could enable company to increase their revenue and reach. Competitive pricing pressure The company may reassess its pricing strategy in case of a competitive environment. By increasing the prices significantly, they may become unattractive to the customers and they may choose companies which offer the same product for a lower price. This reduces customer demand from price sensitives ones could lead to competitive disadvantage and/or result in loss of market share. Raising prices may also lead to a squeezed profit margin. As companies which are consistent in addressing their customers and product profitability are more likely to weather the inflationary cycle (McKinsey, 2022), the brands are recommended to embed a ‘pocket-price-waterfall’ approach in pricing strategies. With this approach, they may use cost optimization e.g., eliminate any waste in production, explore possibility of using different production material, diversify their suppliers etc. So, if the companies are equipped with this, they may maintain their margins proactively without changing their prices. Technology development : Opportunities Sustainability  Companies may start with transforming manufacturing technologies and production process. Adoption of water-saving techniques, reduced chemical usage in textile production, energy efficient production or use of solar panels in factories. As mentioned before, policies such as Net Zero Pakistan collaboration aims to decarbonize the society and National Climate Change Policy of 2021 emphasizes the climate change mitigation goals, thus the use of renewable energy such as solar panels as solar energy is the most attractive alternative solution lately (Business Recorder, 2023).  E-commerce Fashion companies in Pakistan may make use of the e-commerce as an opportunity to grow their business and customers by firstly developing their website by implementing a user interface  (UI) and user experience (UX) design. Attractive UI and UX design are particularly important in fashion business as the customers wish to see an aesthetic and style there. The collective use of UI and UX by a company can improve the customer requirements understanding and help create a more user-friendly interface to improve their satisfaction and provide a positive user experience. Secondly, website maintenance in form of regular updates of the software, bugs or errors prompt resolution and regular monitoring of the company’s website ensures that the website runs smoothly for a seamless user experience. Last but not the least, fashion companies may use the data analytics from their website with use of Google analytics and other tools to gain valuable insight about the performance, behavior of users, traffic, conversion rates etc., therefore tracking the effectiveness of their marketing campaigns and optimize the website according to the data. Coupled with it, the search engine optimization (SEO) practice would enable the website to be ranked higher on the search engine results page (SERP), therefore increasing the traffic on brand’s website.  Increased variety In order to foster creation of variety through the technological development, fashion companies may explore digital technologies such as virtual reality (VR) and augmented reality (AR) for customers to create an immersive experience. As this is not a widely used practice, it may be a great opportunity for companies. It may be used in the following ways by brands (Textile today, 2022): Interactive display screens As after pandemic there was a concern amongst customers and they wished not to try on garments, these mirror-like screens were introduced to help customers visualize what the product would look like without physically trying to product.  Virtual try on By simulating the behavior of textile on the customers body, a virtual application is possible to illustrate how accurately the clothing item will fit the person.  Virtual tailors This service allows customers to get fitted outfits as the machine learning technology provides highly precise measurements of the body.    C ONCLUSION   The goal of this thesis paper was to  provide   managerial implications  for fashion companies in Pakistan in adapting their value chain to align with the customer values.  All  the paper objectives, mentioned in the beginning of this paper, required to achieve this goal were attained .  In order to reach these objectives, data was collected in several stages. Initially, to establish the basis, the term customer value was clearly defined as it served as the fundamental basis of the research paper  in chapter 1 . Following that,  the typology of customer values best suited for this research was identified. The categorization employed was derived extensively from that proposed by Smith and Colgate in their work ‘The Journal of Marketing Theory and Practice’ and included 4 main types: functional, experiential, symbolic and cost values. Subsequently, an analysis to examine their impact on the stages of business value chain was conducted. Thus, a comprehensive analysis was made to explore the key trends and factors impacting customer values, including the impact of COVID-19 pandemic. Through this, 18 key trends were identified and the link was established between them, their reasons, the impact of COVID-19 and stages of business value chain.  After conducting the initial research on the subject matter of customer values, a thorough analysis was made which focused on the value chain of fashion companies in Pakistan in chapter 2. This analysis encompassed examining Pakistan’s fashion industry, including the key features and market characteristics through application of PESTEL analysis. On the next stage of this chapter, a detailed analysis of value chain of fashion companies operating in Pakistan was conducted to gain perspective about the existing value chain. For this purpose, three reputable fashion companies’ representatives in Pakistan were interviewed.  The interview with companies’ representatives supported in attaining comprehension about specific aspects,  comprising of role of several factors on their value  chain stages, including:  sustainability,  demand for innovations, quality, timely deliveries, variety, how is economic instability impacting them and marketing activities and  role of  ecommerce , and  how are they  altering   them  to align with their customer values.   The aim of this was to understand how the value chain could be adapted to align with customer values of fashion companies in Pakistan.  It showed that majority of companies are focusing on aspects of sustainability, quality of their products, providing a variety of products for customers, and as of late have started focusing on e-commerce in their value chain stages.  However, the economic instability in the country has had its impact on the customers and translated in their lowering purchasing power.  In chapter 3, the customer values in Pakistan’s fashion industry were determined. In order to understand the customers values, a questionnaire was made and distributed amongst a sample of 107 customers who were women and men, of age 18-56, employed and had different sources of income. The results preserved the classification of values through typology used earlier in chapter 1 and considered the results in relation to it.  According to the questionnaire, the most important values for customers were  in order of most to least important:  quality ,  price,  variety, sustainability and innovation  with the least significant  in the same order:   conspicuous consumption ,  psychological costs , focus on  self-identify, use of social media for brand awareness and after sale service.  Th erefore , after taking it all into  consideration,  managerial implications  were pr ovided  for the fashion companies in Pakistan for aligning their value  chain with the customer values identified. These  implications  were divided  into 5 stages of value chain: inbound logistics, operations, outbound logistics, marketing and sales and technological development. In these stages there were certain opportunities and  in  some  cases  risks  for the companies presented, which were identified through the market analysis, the survey of customers and the interview results from 3 companies practices examined.  It was identified that with the current market situation and customer values, the aspects which had the most implications for the value chain were the following.  Firstly, as price was a significant value for customers, and the economic conditions in the country have caused reduced disposable income, the implications were demonstrated majorly through management of supply chain in   inbound logistics stage to improve the accuracy of forecasting future demand and enhancement of marketing and sales as more customers are buying during sales promotions.  Secondly, sustainability has to be focused on as the respondents of the questionnaire, namely Gen Z and Millennials, believe that environmentally friendly practices of the companies are important when making purchase decisions. This  implies that fashion companies need to select eco-friendly fabrics in inbound logistics, minimize waste during operations process, use sustainable packaging options in outbound logistics, promote their ecological initiatives through marketing and  implement more energy efficient methods in technological development  stage.  Further to that, with e-commerce gaining popularity since COVID-19 in Pakistan, it illustrates an opportunity for companies to focus on, mainly through automated inventory tracking in inbound logistics for seamless experience of customers, provision of personalized experience in outbound logistics stage, working on making the website user-friendly for marketing and technological development through use of UI and UX designs. These and various other managerial implications were deduced based on the primary and secondary data acquired through this research.  Thus, in order to adapt their value chain better to align with the current customer values, it is crucial for the management of fashion companies in Pakistan to take into consideration  the implications  outlined in this  paper. Цели:
1. Определить предпочтения потребителей одежды в отношении брендов и мест покупки.
2. Исследовать важность различных аспектов при выборе одежды.

Задачи:
1. Составить опросник, включающий вопросы о предпочтениях потребителей одежды в отношении брендов и мест покупки.
2. Анализировать ответы на вопросы опросника с целью определения предпочтений потребителей одежды.
3. Выявлять наиболее важные факторы, влияющие на решение потребителя при выборе одежды.
4. Оценить значимость социальных медиа в процессе принятия решений о покупке одежды.
5. Исследовать интерес потребителей к различным типам контента на социальных медиа, связанных с модой.
